title: jlens autonomous shadow record + auto_outcome candidate v1
description: An autonomous-supervisor shadow record (M10). Carries the same shadow fields as shadow_outcome_v1 PLUS an auto_outcome CANDIDATE object produced by verifiers. auto_outcome is NOT gold and is NEVER promoted without a later human audit. The HUMAN outcome + review_meta fields stay null until a human reviews — the supervisor never writes them. null = unknown/unreviewed (not false). Unknown fields are rejected. This is a SEPARATE schema; the frozen shadow_outcome_v1.json is not modified.

prompt_preview.description: LOCAL-ONLY free text; never committed unredacted

output_preview.description: LOCAL-ONLY free text; never committed unredacted

telemetry_missing.description: true when the endpoint exposed no router-logit telemetry (e.g. GGUF chat serving). When true, policy is null and NO features are fabricated.

task_category.description: optional task tag driving verifier selection (e.g. math, code, exact_answer, current_info)

outcome.description: HUMAN-only outcome fields. The autonomous supervisor NEVER sets these; they stay null until a human reviews.

review_meta.description: HUMAN-only review metadata; the supervisor never sets these.

reviewed_at.description: placeholder string; no wall-clock in this env

auto_outcome.description: Autonomous CANDIDATE outcome from verifiers. NOT gold. Separate namespace from the human outcome. null = the verifiers could not decide.

auto_judged.description: true if any enabled verifier produced a verdict for this task

auto_was_wrong.description: verifier CANDIDATE judgment; null when undecided. Never a gold label.

verifier_confidence.description: aggregate confidence over the verifiers that fired

verifier_evidence_hash.description: stable non-reversible hash of verifier inputs; NEVER raw text

escalate_for_review.description: true when low-confidence, verifier-contradictory, or high-impact — flags the row for later HUMAN review

auto_notes_redacted.description: short redaction-safe note (no prompt/output text); e.g. '[redacted]' or a hashed tag
